content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 25, 1995

Cathy Mattias
Public Utilities Commission
Office of the President
State Building
505 Van Ness Avenue
San Francisco, CA 94102

Re: Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Assistance
Our File No. I-95-140

Dear Ms. Mattias:

You have requested confirmation of telephone assistance
concerning the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act on behalf of Commissioner Daniel Pessler,
whose term with the California Transportation Commission ended on
April 1, 1995.1/ Since your letter is a general inquiry, we
consider your letter to be a request for informal assistance
pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).2/

As we discussed, the conflict of interest code for the
California Transportation Commission (copy enclosed) requires
commissioners to disclose their financial interests under
Disclosure Category 1. Disclosure Category 1 requires disclosure
of "all sources of income, interests in real property, and
investments and business positions". Therefore, Commissioner
Pessler must disclose all reportable investments, interests in
real property, sources of income, and business positions for the
period beginning January 1, 1995 through the date he left office.
The California Transportation Commission's conflict of interest
code does not limit disclosure of travel payments, advances, and
reimbursements to only those pertaining to the California
Transportation Commission.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Ms. Mattias
Our File No. I-95-140
Page Two

     If you have any questions or need further assistance, please
contact me at (916) 322-5660.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Steven G. Churchwell
                                        General Counsel

                                        [Signature: Dixie Howard]
                                        by:  Dixie Howard
                                             Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure

sections.question_synthetic: What conflict of interest disclosure requirements apply to a departing California Transportation Commission commissioner under the Political Reform Act's disclosure provisions?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Commissioner Fessler must disclose all reportable investments, interests in real property, sources of income, and business positions under Disclosure Category 1 for the period from January 1, 1995 through his departure date, with no limitation to Commission-related travel payments, advances, and reimbursements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter (not formal written advice per Regulation 18329(c)). No explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections; content inferred from body text. Document provides confirmation of prior telephone assistance. Disclosure requirements and statutory references clearly stated.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What conflict of interest disclosure requirements apply to a departing California Transportation Commission commissioner under the Political Reform Act's disclosure provisions?

CONCLUSION: Commissioner Fessler must disclose all reportable investments, interests in real property, sources of income, and business positions under Disclosure Category 1 for the period from January 1, 1995 through his departure date, with no limitation to Commission-related travel payments, advances, and reimbursements.

embedding.summary: FPPC provides informal advice confirming that a departing Transportation Commission commissioner must disclose all financial interests under Disclosure Category 1 of the agency's conflict of interest code for the applicable period.
